excerpts.excerpt:
DOCTOR FERRIER
A kiss won’t mean goodbye, but Hello to
love / When the lights go on again --
Seminoff and Poirot come. Ferrier stops, embarrassed.
POIROT
If I may, please. How did the girl die?
OLGA SEMINOFF
The balcony. The canal.
POIROT
A suicide.
OLGA SEMINOFF
It wasn’t her fault. They pushed her to
it.
POIROT
They?
DOCTOR FERRIER
Mrs. Seminoff, please --
OLGA SEMINOFF
What then, Doctor? You cared for her,
you
saw.
EXT. CAMPO. PALAZZO LACRIME DI GIOVANI. NIGHT.

FROM THE IBM.
POIROT PACES, OBSERVING.
HARRY QUIVERS.
ON MS. REYNOLDS. She scans around the tables. Everyone
settled in place. Ready.
MS. REYNOLDS
No one touch me until the trance is
ended. The effects are painful.
Silence. Only the RAIN on the window.
The typewriter’s HUM.
Reynolds lifts her fingers, as if just over typewriter keys.
A pianist at the bench.
MS. REYNOLDS
Alicia Drake. I believe it was you who
called to me. We invite you.
Silence.
Reynolds shudders.
MS. REYNOLDS
Too many spirits. This house is spilling
with the dead. Some souls can’t let go.
DESDEMONA
Do we stop, Ms. Reynolds?
MS. REYNOLDS
(shakes her head, aloud)
If someone wants to be heard we are here.

MAXIME GERARD
I didn’t touch anything.
REYNOLDS SLAPS HERSELF! HER BODY BEGINS TO QUAKE.
SLAP! A SECOND TIME! NAILS DRAWING BLOOD.
Her paroxysm suddenly STOPS. Her body slackens.
FROM THE QUIET... HARRY FLUTTERS... THEN SPEAKS:
HARRY
Alicia...
Harry’s head turns slowly -- looks directly at --
REYNOLDS. HER TIMBRE NOT HER OWN -- IN A YOUNG GIRL’S VOICE:
MS. REYNOLDS
Mamma. Thirsty. So thirsty.
ON POIROT. Trying to comprehend.
ON ROWENA. Pale. Recognizing --
ROWENA DRAKE
Alicia?
MS. REYNOLDS
Hurt -- why -- won’t you leave me -- I
don’t want -- I don’t want to die --
Though Reynolds’ feet do not touch the ground her CHAIR SPINS
AND SPINS AND SPINS --
ROWENA DRAKE
What is it? What’s happening?

POIROT
Her illness.
ROWENA DRAKE
Her mind was on fire. She was like a
little girl again.
ON ROWENA. Overcome. Broken by grief.
INT. ALICIA’S ROOM. PALAZZO. NIGHT.
THE BALCONY DOORS RATTLE. Battered by wind.
Water streams in from a CRACK in the corner.
CUT TO:
CLOSE: A FORK
STABS DOWN --
INT. KITCHEN / PANTRY. PALAZZO. NIGHT.
-- Into CAKE. Seminoff with a heaped plate. Nervous eating.
OLGA SEMINOFF
Oh, I’m a terrible housekeeper. I’m all
she could get. A superstitious city.
POIROT
But you are superstitious too. This
palazzo you believe is haunted, yes?
A pause. Fork down.
OLGA SEMINOFF
Ms. Rowena may own it. But the spirits
possess it.

INT. ANTIQUARIUM. PALAZZO. NIGHT.
The DRAPED CARYATIDS supporting the arch stare out. Water
DRIP-DRIPS onto them.
ON REYNOLDS’ BODY. LAID ON THE TABLE. COVERED IN A SHEET,
BLOOD SOAKED AT THE ABDOMEN.
Doctor Ferrier beside her, finishes his examination.
Nervously offers his assessment --
DOCTOR FERRIER
Jagged weapon, not too sharp. Nails
maybe. No other marks on her. Other
than the obvious.
He accidentally knocks his bag to the floor. He startles.
Quickly ashamed of his hair trigger. He picks it up.
POIROT
Nothing else unusual about her condition?
DOCTOR FERRIER
Impaled on Neoclassical art might be
enough. There’s no such thing as psychic
phenomena if that’s what you’re after.
POIROT
What of the
wrist? You ignore an injury
as well as the precise time of death.
Ferrier looks again. Only now sees the wrist BENT horribly.

DESDEMONA
At midnight? We snuck a drink in the
kitchen. The creepy kid was there.
POIROT
(flips a page of his notepad)
He has you coming in just after the hour.
DESDEMONA
He is mistaken.
POIROT
And how long were you in Ms. Reynolds’
employ?
NICHOLAS
Just over a year. Best year we ever had.
DESDEMONA
We haven’t been hungry since we started
fronting for her.
NICHOLAS
I don’t know what we’ll do without her.
DESDEMONA
We managed before her.

LEOPOLD
Did you hear it?
A pause. Leopold can see from Poirot’s face he alone has.
LEOPOLD
Me and you then. Listening. Wait.
They wait. Nothing.
Wait.
THE PHONE RINGS
Portfoglio picks it up. Taps the receiver. Cold.
VITALE PORTFOGLIO
Still dead.
Poirot takes the phone from him. Listens.
A GENTLE STATIC CRACKLE. THE QUIET SUSURRATION. A WHISPER.
Then gone. He hangs up. When --
A MOVEMENT OF
SHADOW IN THE HALL. A CHILD’S OUTLINE ON THE
WALL. RUNNING --
INT. OUTSIDE MUSIC ROOM. PALAZZO.
Poirot rushes out. Follows the curious SHADOW.
Lost. Maybe never there at all. At which --
A FLURRY OF MOVEMENT AND SHADOW --
FRANTIC FOOTSTEPS --
MOVING INTO THE PIANO NOBILE. Poirot chases -- up --
INT. CLOISTERS CORRIDOR. PALAZZO.

A SEETHING BEEHIVE
FORMED IN THE CHEST OF A LONG-DEAD SMALL SKELETON.
GLISTENING, DRIPPING COMB. RIGID GEOMETRY IN JAGGED BONE.
ALIVE WITH FURIOUS ACTIVITY.
THE DISTURBED SWARM RAGES AND CHURNS. SWIRLING AROUND THEM.
EXT. PALAZZO LACRIME DI GIOVANI. THAT MOMENT.
The pylon finally BREAKS apart -- floats away in pieces, as --
INT. CLOISTERS CORRIDOR. PALAZZO. THAT MOMENT.
THE BOOMING HALTS. Sudden quiet.
Except for Maxime, STRUGGLING. Ferrier pressing his face
closer and closer to the glass.
All frightened of Ferrier. All keeping back.
All but Leopold. Soothing.
LEOPOLD
Dad... Dad, it’s me, I’m right here.
ON FERRIER. Miles and years away.
LEOPOLD
You’re here with me... you see me?
Leopold’s words begin to penetrate. Ferrier’s grip loosens.
Maxime pulls away.
Ferrier realizing what he has done. Leopold holds him.
LEOPOLD
It’s just a thing.
Rowena nods. Watching them, and --
INT. MUSIC ROOM. PALAZZO. NIGHT.

ON ARIADNE. Concern turns to stone. Unrepentant. A new
biting mockery etched. Perhaps her true face, met only now
for the first time.
ARIADNE OLIVER
Oh, won’t you forgive me.
POIROT
Only God can forgive.
ARIADNE OLIVER
Bit of a pickle then.
POIROT
Let us discover how you did it. Baba?
ARIADNE OLIVER
A good touch.
POIROT
The personal invitation to the fiance?
ARIADNE OLIVER
Guaranteed drama with that one. Three
flops, I needed a win. The book’s
already written. Except the ending.
Keeps getting longer.
POIROT
We were friends.

ALICIA’S TEACUP TEETERS
AND FALLS
AND SHATTERS ON THE FLOOR --
POIROT STARES. AT A CUP FALLEN. A child’s prayer answered.
Poirot beyond word or thought, as...
CLOSE ON: A PIECE OF THE BROKEN TEACUP ROLLS...
UNDER THE BED... ROLLS... TO A STOP...
ATOP SOMETHING. Lost under the bed.
PAPER.
He reaches under for it. Brushes off the dust.
CLOSE ON:
HALF A TORN PHOTO. THE OTHER HALF OF MAXIME’S:
THIS HALF SHOWS MAXIME’S JOYFUL FACE. THE GARDEN FULL OF
BRIGHT VIOLET FLOWERS VISIBLE BEHIND HIM.
ON POIROT. STARING AT MAXIME’S PICTURE. SEEING IT. THE
IMPOSSIBLE CONNECTION FORMS. Because of a fallen cup. And --
EXT. PALAZZO LACRIME DI GIOVANI. VENICE. NIGHT.
The violent CANAL WATERS. A stolen glimpse of MOON above
amid storm. Monstrous clouds scud across and devour it.
INT. BOATHOUSE / CAVANA. PALAZZO. NIGHT.
WITH MAXIME. Agitated. Feeling the others’ eyes on him.

Where she asked you to wait... Where she
previously
changed the time --
INSERT CUT: ALONE IN THE MUSIC ROOM, ROWENA ROLLS THE TIME
ON THE METRONOME CLOCK
BACK EIGHT MINUTES -- RUNS OFF --
-- ROWENA RUSHES TO THE CLOSED MUSIC ROOM DOORS, FLUSHED --
-- SHE ENTERS TO MEET SEMINOFF AS THE METRONOME CLOCK ROUNDS
TO MIDNIGHT --
POIROT
Where you could not hear the true
midnight chime.
ARIADNE OLIVER
It read midnight when it was really --
INSERT CUT: THE BROKEN WATCH ON REYNOLDS’ WRIST: 12:04 --
POIROT
After Joyce Reynolds’ death. The only
evidence discarded with a magician’s
touch of misdirection.
